---
title: "Creating Content Queues"
---

To create a Content Queue:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Content Queues tab.
2.  Click + Create Content Queue. The Create Content Queue configuration options are displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Enter a Display Name for the Content Queue. This is how the Content Queue is represented in the Scan and Import and Verify Data interfaces. You may enter a Description for the Content Queue as well.
4.  To assign a user group to the Content Queue, from the Assign user groups list, select the relevant user groups.  Note: Users can view and work only with the Content Queues to which they are assigned. You can assign multiple user groups to a Content Queue. After the assignment, all the users in the assigned groups can work with the Content Queue. If no groups are assigned, the Content Queue is not visible to anyone.
5.  Select the Batch Verification process.
-
If documents are to be manually classified and indexed without verification, select Verify at Scan Time. If at least one document class assigned to the Content Queue has fields assigned to it, users are able to classify and index documents in the Scan and Import interface. If no document class in a Verify at Scan Time Content Queue has fields assigned to it, or if a Verify at Scan Time Content Queue has no document classes associated with it, users are only able to classify documents in the Scan and Import interface.

-
If users are to manually classify and index documents, and the documents are to undergo verification, select Verify from Batch List. Users may classify documents in either the Scan and Import or Verify Data interfaces, and users index documents in the Verify Data interface.

Tip:
A Verify at Scan Time Content Queue is well-suited for scenarios in which indexing is to be completed quickly by the same user who acquired the documents. A Verify from Batch List Content Queue can be used in scenarios in which indexing is to be completed later in the process, potentially by a different user.

6.  Configure the options specific to each process flow type:
-
If you selected Verify at Scan Time, and users are to be able to search text on exported PDFs, select Include searchable text on exported PDF.

-
If you selected If you selected Verify from Batch List, continue to step 10.

7.  To add the appropriate bar code separation profiles to the Content Queue, click Add Profile. The Add Bar Code Separation Profile dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
8.  Select one or more bar code separation profiles to add to the Content Queue and click Add X Profiles, where X is the number of profiles you selected. The profiles are added to the Content Queue and the Add Bar Code Separation Profile dialog box is closed.  Note:
The same bar code separation profile can be added to multiple Content Queues.

9.  To reorder the bar code separation profiles associated with the Content Queue, click the dots icon to the left of the profile you want to move and drag the profile to a new position in the list of profiles.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a profile is positioned in the list on the Content Queue, the greater priority is given to the profile. This priority determines which profile to use for page separation when multiple profiles are detected on a single page during a batch capture process.

10.  To add the appropriate document classes to the Content Queue, click Add Class. The Add Classes dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
11.  Select one or more document classes to add to the Content Queue and click Add X Classes, where X is the number of document classes you selected. The document classes are added to the Content Queue and the Add Classes dialog box is closed.
12.  To reorder the classes associated with the Content Queue, click the dots icon to the left of the class you want to move and drag the class to a new position in the list of classes.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a class is positioned in the list on the Content Queue, the greater priority is given to the class. This priority determines the order of class assignment during bar code classification when multiple classes are detected for a single document.

13.  To view the class details, click the class name.  Note: To modify the class details in the Classes page, click Go to class.
14.  To configure which fields on a document class are required for indexing:
1.  Click the Field Options button of the corresponding document class. The field options dialog box for the corresponding document class is displayed.
2.  For all fields that are required, select the Required option.
3.  Click OK. The field options dialog box is closed.
15.  Click Okay.
16.  Click Save.
